# The Baker of Bellwether Lane

In Bellwether Lane they swore Boxwell was a baker, and always had been. Hadn't
he stood at the ovens before dawn? Hadn't the loaves come out singing? The
flour on his sleeves seemed proof enough for anyone who asked.

No one in Bellwether Lane had ever seen the inside of his cedar case, and no
one thought to wonder why a baker would carry one.
